observed_overlap: Can have exactly the same pronoun–classifier–noun surface order.
decisive_difference: Possessive `N1` and `N2` denote distinct possessor and possessed referents; target `N1` and `N2` are coreferential.

observed_overlap: Can express a broad nominal association or possession.
decisive_difference: Does not require the bare classifier frame and does not encode identity between `N1` and `N2`.

observed_overlap: Some coreferential N–CL–N tokens are non-argument vocatives and address the referent denoted by both nominals.
decisive_difference: Tse and Chin report that more than 65% of coreferential tokens are arguments; vocative function is only one profile and the frame is not limited to the current lexicalized inventory.
Does not itself establish whether a preceding pronoun is possessor or coreferential nominal.
